name: SCADA HMI Takeover
description: Attack tree modelling compromise of a SCADA Human-Machine Interface to display false process data while silently altering physical operations
root_objective: Take over SCADA HMI to display false readings while manipulating physical processes

nodes.title: Takeover SCADA HMI for Covert Process Manipulation
nodes.description: Compromise the HMI to display fabricated process data to operators while silently altering real process parameters

nodes.title: Gain Access to HMI Host System
nodes.description: Obtain interactive or remote access to the workstation running HMI software

nodes.title: Exploit Unpatched HMI Operating System
nodes.description: Many HMI stations run legacy Windows (7/XP/Server 2008) with no security patches applied

nodes.title: Compromise VNC/RDP Remote Access
nodes.description: Exploit weak or default credentials on remote desktop services commonly left enabled on HMI stations

nodes.title: Infect via USB Drive
nodes.description: Deliver malware via USB at the physical HMI station, bypassing air-gapped network controls

nodes.title: Manipulate HMI Display Layer
nodes.description: Alter what operators see on the HMI screens to show normal readings during manipulation

nodes.title: Modify HMI Project Files
nodes.description: Alter the HMI project configuration (e.g. WinCC, FactoryTalk) to change alarm thresholds and display ranges

nodes.title: Inject False OPC-UA Tag Values
nodes.description: Intercept and modify OPC-UA data tags between the SCADA server and HMI client to display fabricated values

nodes.title: Send Malicious Commands to Field Devices
nodes.description: Issue unauthorized commands to PLCs/RTUs while the HMI shows normal operation

nodes.title: Send Direct Modbus/DNP3 Commands
nodes.description: Bypass the HMI and send process commands directly to field devices via industrial protocols

nodes.title: Modify PLC Setpoints via Engineering Software
nodes.description: Use stolen engineering credentials to alter PLC setpoints through legitimate programming software

nodes.title: Suppress Alarms and Historian Logs
nodes.description: Prevent alarm systems and data historians from recording the true process state

nodes.title: Disable or Modify Alarm Configuration
nodes.description: Raise alarm thresholds or disable critical alarms so operators are not alerted to dangerous conditions

nodes.title: Tamper with Process Historian
nodes.description: Modify or delete historian data to erase evidence of process manipulation
